Rahu and Śani in the 11th will indicate the destruction of elder brothers and/or sisters and in the 3rd younger ones. 32. If Śukr is in the 3rd, or the 11th from Lagn Pad, there would have been an abortion to the mother earlier. Same is the effect, if Śukr is in the 8th from natal Lagn, or from Lagn Pad. 33-36. These are the effects, o Brahmin, as stated by Maharishis for the 3rd and the 11th from Lagn Pad. Should Candr, Guru, Budh and Mangal be in the 3rd, or the 11th from Lagn Pad,

there will be many valorous co-born. Should Śani and Mangal be in the 3rd, or the 11th from Lagn Pad, or give Drishtis thereto, younger and elder co-born will, respectively, be destroyed. If Śani is alone in one of the said Bhavas, the native will be spared, while the co-born will die. Ketu in the 3rd, or the 11th will give abundant happiness from one‟s sisters. 37. Other Matters from Lagn Pad (up to Sloka 43). If the 6th from Lagn Pad is occupied by a malefic and is bereft of a Yuti with, or a Drishti from a benefic, the native will be a thief. 38. If Rahu is in the 7th, or the 12th from Lagn Pad, or gives a Drishti to one of the said Bhavas, the native will be endowed with spiritual knowledge and be very fortunate. 39. If Budh is in Lagn Pad, the native will Lord over a whole country, while Guru will make him a knower of all things. Śukr in this context denotes a poet/speaker (also see Ch. 29, verse 30). 40. O excellent of the Brahmins, if benefics occupy the 2nd from Upa Pad, or from Lagn Pad, the native will be endowed with all kinds of wealth and be intelligent. 41. One will surely become a thief, if the Lord of the 2nd from Upa Pad is in Dhan Bhava and is there yuti with a malefic Grah. 42-43. O Brahmin, if Rahu is in the 2nd from the Lord of the 7th, counted from Upa Pad, the native will have long and projected teeth. Ketu in the 2nd from the Lord of the 7th, counted from Upa Pad, will cause stammering and Śani in the 2nd from the Lord of the 7th, counted from Upa Pad, will make one look ugly. Mixed will be the effects, if there are mixed Grahas. Ch. 31. Argala, or Intervention from Grahas 1. O Maharishi Parashar, you have told of auspicious effects, related to Argala. Kindly narrate its conditions and effects. 2-9. Formation of Argala. Maitreya, I explain below Argala to know the

definite effects of Bhavas and Grahas. Grahas in the 4th, 2nd and the 11th cause Argalas, while obstructors of the Argala will be those in the 10th, 12th and 3rd from a Bhava, or a Grah. If the Argala causing Grah is stronger than the obstructing one, the former will prevail. Or, if the number of Argalas are more than the obstructing Grahas, then also the Argala will prevail. If there are 3, or more malefics in the 3rd they will cause Vipreet Argala (more effective intervention), which will also be harmless and be very favourable. The 5th is also an Argala place, while the Grah in the 9th will counteract such Argala. As Rahu and Ketu have retrograde motions, the Argalas and obstructions be also counted accordingly in a reverse manner. Maharishis say, that the Argala, caused by one Grah, will yield limited effect, by two medium and by more than two, excellent effects. Argalas should be counted from a Rāśi, or a Grah, as the case may be. The Argala, which is unobstructed will be fruitful, while the one duly obstructed will go astray. The Argala effects will be derived in the Dasha periods of the Rāśi, or Grah concerned. Notes. „Argala‟ in Sanskrit is figuratively used to denote an impediment, or obstruction. Some suggest, that the Argala obstruction places are countable from the Argala place instead of from the original place, or Grah. This is not logical and a glance into Gochar Vedha (obstructions during transits) will confirm our findings. 10. Special. The Argala, caused by placement of a Grah in the first one fourth part of the Rāśi, is countered by another, placed in the 4th quarter of the respective obstructive Rāśi. Similarly 2nd quarter‟s Argala is eliminated by the 3rd quarter placement of another Grah. Notes. If the Argala causing Grah and the obstructing Grah are in the respective quarters, the obstruction will come to pass. Otherwise not. 11-17. Argala Effects. Should there be Argala for the Arudh Pad, for the natal Lagn and for the 7th from both, the native will be famous and

fortunate. A malefic, or a benefic, causing unobstructed Argala, giving a Drishti to Lagn will make one famous. Similarly a malefic, or a benefic, causing unobstructed Argala, giving a Drishti to Dhan Bhava denotes acquisition of wealth and grains, to Sahaj Bhava happiness from co-born, to Bandhu Bhava residences, quadrupeds and relatives, to Putr Bhava sons, grand sons and intelligence, to Ari Bhava fear from enemies, to Yuvati Bhava abundant wealth and marital happiness, to Randhr Bhava difficulties, to Dharm Bhava fortunes, to Karm Bhava royal honour, to Labh Bhava gains and to Vyaya Bhava expenses. The Argala by benefics will give various kinds of happiness, while benefic effects will be meddling with malefic Argalas. Argala by both benefics and malefics will yield results. Notes. 1. Argala can be caused by a benefic, which is known, as Subh Argala. This Argala can be from a malefic also, so that the benefic, causing Argala, stalls the malefic role. If the benefic‟s Argala is obstructed by another, then the benefic will become ineffective in Argala and the first- mentioned malefic will operate freely. 2. Argala can be by a malefic with reference to a benefic, so that the native does not enjoy good effects, due to the benefic. This is Pap (malefic) Argala. If the Argala is eliminated by a benefic, or a malefic, then the first mentioned benefic will be at liberty to act, according to his own disposition. 18. Should there be (unobstructed) Argala for Lagn, Putr and Dharm Bhava, the native will doubtlessly become a king and fortunate. Ch. 32. Karakatwas of the Grahas 1-2. I now detail below Atma Karak etc., obtainable from among the 7 Grahas, viz. Sūrya to Śani. Some say, that Rahu will become a Karak, when there is a state of similarity in terms of longitude between (two) Grahas. Yet some say, that the 8 Grahas, including Rahu, will have to be considered irrespective of such a state. 3-8. Atma Karak Defined. Among the Grahas from Sūrya etc. whichever has traversed maximum number of degrees in a particular Rāśi is called Atma Karak. If

the degrees are identical, then the one with more minutes of arc and, if the minutes are also identical, then the one with higher seconds of arc, have to be considered. In that case these three are called Anthya Karak, Madhya Karak and Upakheta. In the case of Rahu deduct his longitude in that particular Rāśi from 30. The Karakas will have to be decided, as above and, as per further rules given below. Out of these Karakas, Atma Karak is the most important and has a prime say on the native, just as the king is the most famous among the men of his country and is the head of all affairs and is entitled to arrest and release men. 9-12. Importance of Atma Karak. O Brahmin, as the minister cannot go against the king, the other Karakas, viz. Putr Karak, Amatya Karak etc. cannot predominate over Atma Karak in the affairs of the native. If the Atma Karak is adverse, other Karakas cannot give their benefic effects. Similarly, if Atma Karak is favourable, other Karakas cannot predominate with their malefic influences. 13-17. Other Karakas. The Grah next to Atma Karak in terms of longitude is called Amatya Karak. Similarly following one another in terms of longitude are Bhratru Karak, Matru Karak, Pitru Karak, Putr Karak, Gnati Karak and Stri Karak. These are Char Karakas, or inconstant significators. Some consider Matru Karak and Putr Karak, as identical. If two Grahas have the same longitude, both become the same Karak, in which case there will be a deficit of one Karak. In that circumstance consider constant significator in the context of benefic/malefic influence for the concerned relative. 18-21. Constant Karakatwas. I narrate below the constant Karakatwas, as related to the Grahas. The stronger among Sūrya and Śukr indicates the father, while the stronger among Candr and Mangal indicates the mother. Mangal denotes sister, brother-in-law, younger brother and mother. Budh rules maternal relative, while Guru indicates paternal grand father. Husband and sons are, respectively, denoted by Śukr and Śani. From Ketu note wife, father, mother, parents-in law and maternal grand father.

These are constant Karakatwas. 22-24. Bhavas Related. These constant significances are derivable from the Bhavas, counted from the said constant Karakatwas. The 9th from Sūrya denotes father, the 4th from Candr mother, the 3rd from Mangal brothers, the 6th from Budh maternal uncle, the 5th from Guru sons, the 7th from Śukr wife and the 8th from Śani death. The learned should consider all these and declare related effects accordingly. 25-30. Yog Karakas. O Brahmin, I make below a passing reference to Yog Karakas (mutual co-workers). Grahas become Yog Karakas, if they are in mutual angles identical with own Rāśis, exaltation Rāśis, or friendly Rāśis. In Karm Bhava a Grah will be significantly so. Grahas simply (not being in friendly, own, or exaltation Rāśis) in Lagn, Bandhu and Yuvati Bhava do not become such Yog Karakas. Even, if they be placed in other Bhavas, but with such dignities, as mentioned, shall become Yog Karakas. With such Grahas even a person of mean birth will become a king and be affluent. One born of royal scion, then will surely become a king. Thus the effects be declared, considering the number of such Grahas and the order the native belongs to. 31-34. Bhava Significance. I now narrate the significance of the Bhavas. Tanu Bhava denotes the soul (self), Dhan family, finance, wife etc., Sahaj younger brothers/sisters, Putr progeny and Yuvati wife. It is also said, that a Grah in Putr becomes a Karak for wife. The Karakatwas of the Bhava in order are Sūrya, Guru, Mangal, Candr, Guru, Mangal, Śukr, Śani, Guru, Budh, Guru and Śani. 35-37. O excellent of the Brahmins, after knowing the merits of Tanu Bhava etc. the good and bad effects can be declared. Ari, Randhr and Vyaya are Trikas, Dusthan, or malefic Bhavas. Sahaj, Ari, Karm and Labh are Upachayas. Dhan, Putr, Randhr and Labh are Panapharas and Sahaj, Ari, Dharm and Vyaya are Apoklimas. Association with Trikas will inflict evils. Kendras and Konas (Putr and Dharm) are auspicious Bhavas, the association with which turns even evil into auspiciousness. (Also see Ch. 34 for more information) Ch.

33. Effects of Karakāńś 1. O Brahmin, as laid down by Lord Brahma, I now tell you about the effects of Karakāńś identical with Mesh etc. (Karakāńś is the Navāńś, occupied by the Atma Karak Grah) 2-8. Karakāńś in Various Rāśis. If Atma Karak happens to be in Mesh Navāńś, there will be nuisance from rats and cats at all times. A malefic joining will further increase the nuisance. Should Atma Karak be in Vrishabh Navāńś, happiness from quadrupeds will result. Should Atma Karak be in Mithun Navāńś, the native will be afflicted by itch etc. Should Atma Karak be in Kark Navāńś, there will be fear from water etc. If Atma Karak happens to be in Simh Navāńś, fear will be from tiger etc. If Atma Karak happens to be in Kanya Navāńś, itch, corpulence, fire etc. will cause trouble, while, if Atma Karak is in Tula Navāńś, he will make one a trader and skilful in making robes etc. Vrischik Navāńś, holding Atma Karak, will bring troubles from snakes etc. and also affliction to mother‟s breasts. There will be falls from height and conveyances etc., if it is Dhanu Navāńś, that is occupied by Atma Karak. Makar Navāńś in this respect denotes gains from water dwelling beings and conch, pearl, coral etc. If it is Kumbh Navāńś, holding Atma Karak, the native will construct tanks etc. And in Meen Navāńś the Atma Karak will grant final emancipation. The Drishti of a benefic will remove evils, while that of a malefic will cause no good. 9-11. O Brahmin, if there be only benefics in Karakāńś and the Navāńś of Lagn receives a Drishti from a benefic, the native will undoubtedly become a king. Should the Kendras/Konas from the Karakāńś be occupied by benefics, devoid of malefic association, the native will be endowed with wealth and learning. The combination of benefic and malefic influence will in this context yield mixed results. If the Upakheta (UpaGrah, vide Ch.32

The grahas act as karmic agents: Sūrya signifies Ātman and temporal authority; Chandra signifies mind and receptivity; Kuja signifies force and incision; Budha signifies discrimination; Guru signifies wisdom and grace; Śukra signifies union and craft; Śani signifies delay and structure; Rāhu and Ketu signify eclipse axes of obsession and release. Marriage matching considers the Moon's rāśi and nakṣatra relationship between partners, including the aṣṭakūṭa tally of varṇa, vaśya, tara, yoni, graha maitri, gaṇa, bhakūṭa, and nāḍi — never read points without the full chart context.

## Verse 1-6: First Bhava (Lagna / Ascendant) **BPHS 34.1** — The first bhava (Lagna) represents the body, complexion, strength, vigour, happiness, and the nature of the native. The lagna lord's placement, dignity, and aspects determine the overall quality of life. **BPHS 34.2** — The first house governs the physical appearance, general constitution, longevity, and the self (atman). It is the most powerful of all bhavas — called the angular house of houses. When the lagna is strong and occupied or aspected by benefics, the native is endowed with good health, confidence, and success. **BPHS 34.3** — The Lagna lord in a Kendra (1, 4, 7, 10) or Trikona (1, 5, 9) gives strength, fame, prosperity, and general well-being throughout life. The Lagna lord in dusthanas (6, 8, 12) causes obstruction to happiness and physical challenges. **BPHS 34.4** — Natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, strong Moon) in the Lagna ward off diseases, grant intelligence, and strengthen all aspects of life. Natural malefics (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) in the Lagna without aspect of benefics can cause physical marks, impulsiveness, or health challenges. **BPHS 34.5** — The Lagna is also called the house of self-knowledge (Atma-bhava). The strength of the Lagna lord determines how well the native can manifest their soul's purpose during the dasha period of that planet. **BPHS 34.6** — The first house corresponds to Aries in the natural zodiac order; its lord Mars naturally governs vitality and drive. When Aries is the lagna, Mars rules both as lagna lord and governs vitality. ## Verse 7-12: Second Bhava (Dhana / Wealth House) **BPHS 34.7** — The second bhava governs accumulated wealth (dhana), family (kutumba), speech and communication, face and eyes, dietary habits, and the right eye specifically. The second house is called the Dhana-bhava or Kutumba-bhava. **BPHS 34.8** — The second lord strongly placed or in its own sign brings prosperity, a large family, sweet speech, and fine eyesight. Planets in the 2nd house influence speech — Jupiter gives eloquence; Mercury gives articulate, clever speech; Venus gives melodious speech. **BPHS 34.9** — Saturn or Rahu in the second house

can delay accumulation of wealth, cause speech difficulties, or bring family separations. However, if these planets are well-placed or receive benefic aspects, they may give delayed but sustained wealth. **BPHS 34.10** — The second house also governs fixed assets and savings. Its connection with the 11th house (gains) and 5th/9th (dharmic money) through their lords creates Dhana Yogas — combinations for wealth. **BPHS 34.11** — The maraka (death-inflicting) function of the 2nd house: the 2nd lord as a maraka can bring physical challenges during its dasha when combined with adversarial house lordship. **BPHS 34.12** — A strong and well-placed second lord, combined with the 11th lord, in a Kendra or Trikona creates powerful wealth combinations. The native accumulates resources steadily and finds family life supportive. ## Verse 13-18: Third Bhava (Sahaja / Siblings/Courage) **BPHS 34.13** — The third bhava governs courage (parakrama), younger siblings (sahaja), short travels, communications, writing, and the right ear. The natural significator (karaka) of the third is Mars (courage) and Mercury (communications/writing). **BPHS 34.14** — The third lord well-placed gives bold initiative, active younger siblings, success in writing, trading, and short journeys. Mars in the third house (except for certain lagnas) gives courage, enterprising spirit, and physical vitality. **BPHS 34.15** — The third house governs the arms, shoulders, and hands. Malefics in the third can cause injuries to these limbs, but also often give physical strength and martial ability. **BPHS 34.16** — For Vedic timing purposes, the third house rules the 3-month period after the primary dasha period activates. Short travels and communications are most fruitful when the third lord or its karaka (Mars/Mercury) is well-aspected by transits. ## Verse 19-24: Fourth Bhava (Sukha / Happiness House) **BPHS 34.17** — The fourth bhava governs happiness (sukha), mother, home, land, property, vehicles, and comforts. The natural significator of the fourth is Moon (mother) and Mercury (education). Jupiter aspects or placement in the fourth greatly enhances general happiness. **BPHS 34.18** — The fourth lord's placement and strength determine the quality of the home environment, relationship with the mother, and accumulation of property. The fourth lord in a Kendra

or Trikona gives a good home, happiness, and a nurturing mother. **BPHS 34.19** — Saturn in the fourth (unless it rules the fourth through Libra or Capricorn rising) can delay property acquisition, cause separation from mother, or bring emotional challenges. However, Saturn in its own sign in the fourth gives eventual property accumulation through patience. **BPHS 34.20** — Dig Bala (directional strength) — Moon and Venus gain directional strength in the fourth house (North direction). Their presence here amplifies happiness, family harmony, and property blessings significantly. **BPHS 34.21** — The fourth house governs the chest region of the body, including the lungs and heart. Afflictions to the fourth can indicate respiratory or cardiac challenges; benefic planets here strengthen these organs. **BPHS 34.22** — The fourth house is a Kendra house (angular house). A Yogakaraka planet placed here generates strong Raja Yoga effects, elevating the native's status and material happiness. ## Verse 25-30: Fifth Bhava (Putra / Intelligence House) **BPHS 34.23** — The fifth bhava governs children (putra), intelligence (buddhi), past-life merit (poorva punya), creativity, speculation, mantras, and devotional practice. The natural significator of the fifth is Jupiter. **BPHS 34.24** — The fifth lord or Jupiter strongly placed in the fifth creates Putra Yoga — blessings from children and intelligence. Jupiter in the fifth house is an excellent position, amplifying all fifth house significations through its own natural rulership. **BPHS 34.25** — The fifth house governs the stomach and upper digestive system. A strong fifth lord and natural fifth house significator (Jupiter) supports digestive health and metabolic vitality. **BPHS 34.26** — The fifth is a Trikona house (trine) — eternally auspicious. Lords of the fifth are always functionally benefic for any lagna, regardless of their natural status. A planet simultaneously ruling a Kendra and the fifth constitutes a powerful Yogakaraka. **BPHS 34.27** — Affliction to the fifth house — through Saturn, Rahu, or the sixth/eighth/twelfth lords — can delay conception, cause difficulties with children's health, or weaken the concentration needed for creative and speculative endeavors. **BPHS 34.28** — The fifth house in Vedic tradition represents Poorva Punya — the accumulated merit

(punyas) from past lives. Strong fifth lords often indicate a person of refined spiritual sensibility and natural intuition. ## Verse 31-36: Sixth Bhava (Shatru / Enemies and Disease) **BPHS 34.29** — The sixth bhava governs enemies, disease, debt, maternal relatives, service, and litigation. The natural significator is Mars (for enemies and surgeries) and Saturn (for chronic disease and service). **BPHS 34.30** — The sixth lord placed in a dusthana (6, 8, 12) or aspected by natural malefics causes difficulties from enemies and disease. However, the sixth lord in the sixth itself (own house placement) can become a Viparita Raja Yoga condition, ultimately turning obstacles into triumphs. **BPHS 34.31** — The sixth house governs the lower digestive system, intestines, and kidneys. Afflictions here through malefics indicate chronic digestive issues or urinary problems. A strong sixth lord protects health through disciplined lifestyle practices. **BPHS 34.32** — The sixth house represents service (seva). Jupiter in the sixth house, despite being uncomfortable, often creates a counselor, healer, or one who elevates others through service. The sixth house placement is sometimes called the position of the servant-leader. ## Verse 37-42: Seventh Bhava (Kalatra / Partnership House) **BPHS 34.33** — The seventh bhava governs marriage, partnerships, business associates, foreign travel, and the spouse (kalatra). The natural significator of the seventh is Venus (for marriage) and Jupiter (for wisdom in partnerships). **BPHS 34.34** — The seventh lord in a Kendra or Trikona, or in its own sign, gives a supportive, compatible spouse and successful business partnerships. Venus in the seventh in a benefic sign gives a beautiful, harmonious spouse and pleasurable married life. **BPHS 34.35** — Saturn in the seventh can delay marriage but once married, gives stability and a long-lasting relationship. Rahu in the seventh creates unconventional, complex partnerships and foreign connections. **BPHS 34.36** — The seventh house is a Kendra and governs the reproductive organs and lower back. Its strength determines vitality in partnerships and reproductive health. **BPHS 34.37** — The seventh is also a Maraka (death-inflicting) house. The seventh lord can operate as a maraka during periods of health challenges, especially when it rules

only dusthana houses for the given lagna. ## Verse 43-48: Eighth Bhava (Ayur / Longevity House) **BPHS 34.38** — The eighth bhava governs longevity (ayur), occult sciences, sudden events, inheritance, chronic illness, transformation, and hidden matters. The natural significator is Saturn (longevity and chronic conditions). **BPHS 34.39** — The eighth lord in a dusthana can shorten life, create sudden reversals, or bring heavy karmic lessons. However, benefics in the eighth house — especially Jupiter — can give interest in occult sciences and sometimes unexpected wealth from inheritances. **BPHS 34.40** — The eighth house represents the shadow of karma — what we must experience and transform in this lifetime. Its influence is intense, transformative, and unavoidable during the period of its lord's dasha. **BPHS 34.41** — The eighth house governs the genitals, rectum, and excretory organs. Malefic influence here without benefic mitigation can cause reproductive or eliminatory challenges. ## Verse 49-54: Ninth Bhava (Bhagya / Fortune House) **BPHS 34.42** — The ninth bhava governs fortune (bhagya), father, guru, dharma, higher education, long journeys, philosophy, and divine grace. The natural significator is Jupiter (for dharma, wisdom, and good fortune) and Sun (for the father). **BPHS 34.43** — The ninth lord in a Kendra creates the most powerful form of Raja Yoga — when combined with a Kendra lord, this gives unprecedented fortune, fame, and elevation in life. The native with a strongly placed ninth lord is said to receive divine blessings in all endeavors. **BPHS 34.44** — Jupiter in the ninth house is exalted in its natural domain — giving great faith, philosophical wisdom, generous fortune, a wise and fortunate father, and access to the highest teachings. This is one of the most auspicious planetary placements possible. **BPHS 34.45** — The ninth house is a Trikona of Lakshmi — the house of divine fortune and the favor of natural law. Its lord and occupants receive the inherent auspiciousness of the trine's alignment with cosmic dharma. **BPHS 34.46** — Affliction to the ninth house — through its lord being in a dusthana, or through malefics with no benefic aspect — can create challenges

with father, loss of fortune suddenly, or a drift away from one's dharmic path. ## Verse 55-60: Tenth Bhava (Karma / Career House) **BPHS 34.47** — The tenth bhava governs career (karma), status, authority, government, public reputation, and the father (in some traditions). The natural significator is Saturn (for profession and discipline), Sun (for authority and government). **BPHS 34.48** — Dig Bala (directional strength) — Sun and Mars gain directional strength in the tenth house (South direction). The Sun strongly placed in the tenth gives rise to government favor, leadership, and distinguished career. **BPHS 34.49** — The tenth lord exalted or in its own sign creates powerful Karma Yoga — the native rises to distinguished positions through their own efforts. The tenth lord with Yogakaraka creates Raja Yoga of the highest order. **BPHS 34.50** — Saturn in the tenth house, especially for lagnas where Saturn rules a trikona, gives slow but powerful career rise, discipline in public life, and longevity in career positions. **BPHS 34.51** — The tenth house governs the knees and joints. Saturn afflicting the tenth can manifest as knee problems or joint issues during its dasha periods. ## Verse 61-66: Eleventh Bhava (Labha / Gains House) **BPHS 34.52** — The eleventh bhava governs gains (labha), income, elder siblings, fulfillment of desires, and social networks. The natural significator of the eleventh is Jupiter (for gains and abundance). **BPHS 34.53** — The eleventh lord in a Kendra or Trikona gives steady, increasing income, multiple sources of wealth, and many friends and supporters. Jupiter in the eleventh is particularly auspicious, bringing financial gains through knowledge, teaching, and wisdom. **BPHS 34.54** — Even malefic planets in the eleventh house tend to give material gains — this is the house where their driving energy becomes productive for material accumulation. Saturn's discipline in the eleventh yields long-term financial gains through structured effort. **BPHS 34.55** — The eleventh house governs the left ear and left shoulder. Its strength or affliction often indicates the nature of one's social relationships and the ease or difficulty in achieving desired goals. ## Verse 67-72: Twelfth Bhava (Vyaya /

Loss House) **BPHS 34.56** — The twelfth bhava governs losses (vyaya), expenditure, foreign lands, spiritual liberation (moksha), hidden enemies, and sleep. The natural significator of the twelfth is Saturn (isolation, loss) and Ketu (liberation). **BPHS 34.57** — The twelfth lord in a dusthana (Viparita position) creates hidden benefit through apparent loss — the native ultimately wins through situations that first seem like defeats or expenses. **BPHS 34.58** — Benefics in the twelfth house — especially Jupiter or Venus — give spiritual inclination, comfort in foreign lands, charitable nature, and peaceful sleep. These natives often have secret supporters or find their best work done in retreat, solitude, or abroad. **BPHS 34.59** — Rahu in the twelfth house gives strong desire for foreign travel, spiritual practice, and sometimes addictive tendencies. Ketu in the twelfth gives natural inclination toward moksha (liberation), spiritual bypassing of material concerns, or final-life themes. **BPHS 34.60** — The twelfth house governs the left eye and feet. Afflictions here can cause eye strain, foot problems, or disturbed sleep. A strong twelfth lord and its benefic association gives deeply restful sleep and freedom from hidden enemies.

topic: Combinations for Wealth, Luck, and Achievement (Key Yogas) (part 1)
text: ## Gajakesari Yoga **BPHS 36.1** — If Jupiter is in a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house) from the Moon, Gajakesari Yoga is formed. The native will be splendorous, wealthy, intelligent, endowed with many laudable virtues, and will please the king. This yoga bestows lasting fame that endures beyond one's lifetime. **BPHS 36.2** — The strength of Gajakesari Yoga depends on the dignity of both Jupiter and the Moon. When both are strong (exalted or in own signs), the effects are extraordinary — the native becomes a leader in their field with international recognition. When one or both are weakened by debilitation or combustion, the effects are correspondingly reduced. **BPHS 36.3** — Gajakesari Yoga must be computed from the Moon's house in the natal chart, not from the Lagna. Jupiter in Kendra FROM MOON is the classical definition. A Jupiter in the 1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th from the Moon constitutes this yoga, regardless of the Lagna position. **BPHS 36.4** — The native with Gajakesari Yoga, when fully operative, will be eloquent in speech, will be respected by learned scholars, will be known for righteous conduct, will accumulate wealth through legitimate means, and will perform deeds remembered by posterity. ## Budhaditya Yoga **BPHS 36.5** — When Mercury conjoins the Sun in the same sign and Mercury is not combust (beyond 14 degrees from the Sun), Budhaditya Yoga arises. The native becomes highly intelligent, skillful in arts and sciences, a good speaker, and earns respect in scholarly circles. **BPHS 36.6** — Budhaditya Yoga particularly enhances analytical abilities, communication skills, and commercial acumen. It also bestows keen judgment, the ability to manage financial affairs successfully, and a natural talent for teaching and explaining complex subjects. **BPHS 36.7** — The strength of Budhaditya Yoga increases when the conjunction occurs in the signs where Sun and Mercury are strong — Aries (Sun exalted), Virgo (Mercury exalted and own sign), Gemini (Mercury own sign), or Leo (Sun own sign). Placement in Kendra or Trikona further amplifies this yoga. **BPHS 36.8** — When Mercury is within 14 degrees of the Sun (combust zone), Budhaditya Yoga

topic: Combinations for Wealth, Luck, and Achievement (Key Yogas) (part 2)
text: weakens significantly. The intelligence and communication abilities are present but the native may struggle to express them clearly, or may have blind spots in judgment related to the Sun's domain (ego, authority figures). ## Chandra-Mangal Yoga **BPHS 36.9** — When Moon and Mars are in mutual conjunction or mutual aspect (Mars in kendra from Moon or Moon from Mars), Chandra-Mangal Yoga forms. This yoga gives a sharp, emotionally driven ambition and strong earning capacity, particularly through property, real estate, or professions requiring courage. **BPHS 36.10** — When Chandra-Mangal Yoga is well-placed — Moon and Mars both in good dignity (own or exaltation signs) — this yoga produces wealth and initiative. The native demonstrates exceptional drive toward goals and an ability to mobilize resources and people. **BPHS 36.11** — When afflicted, this yoga can cause rashness, emotional volatility, strained maternal relations, or a tendency toward impulsive financial decisions. The native must learn to channel martial energy productively. ## Adhi Yoga **BPHS 36.12** — When natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury) occupy the 6th, 7th, and 8th houses from the Moon simultaneously, Adhi Yoga is formed. The native rises to political or administrative authority, enjoying the role of a minister, general, or chief executive. **BPHS 36.13** — Different strength variations of Adhi Yoga depend on how many benefics are placed in these positions. With three benefics in the 6th, 7th, and 8th from Moon: the highest Adhi Yoga gives rise to a ruler or head of state. With two benefics: ministerial rank. With one: a commander or executive. ## Kemal Adhruma Yoga (Full Moon Configuration) **BPHS 36.14** — When no planet is in the 2nd or 12th house from the Moon (Kemadruma Yoga), the native experiences emotional instability, lack of mental peace, and difficulties accumulating permanent wealth. Kemadruma cancels when Jupiter is in a Kendra from the Moon or Lagna, or when the Moon joins another planet. **BPHS 36.15** — Kemadruma Yoga is cancelled (Kemadruma Bhanga) when: (a) a planet occupies the 2nd or 12th from Moon, (b) a planet occupies the 2nd or 12th from Lagna, (c) Moon is in a Kendra

topic: Combinations for Wealth, Luck, and Achievement (Key Yogas) (part 3)
text: from Lagna, (d) Moon conjoins or is aspected by a benefic. In these cases, the negative effects are nullified. ## Vasumati Yoga **BPHS 36.16** — When natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury) occupy upachaya houses (3rd, 6th, 10th, 11th) from the Moon or Lagna, Vasumati Yoga is formed. The native accumulates great wealth over time, particularly after the age of 35-40 when upachaya houses mature. ## Parivartana Yoga (Exchange Yoga) **BPHS 36.17** — When two planets exchange signs — meaning each is placed in the other's owned sign — a Parivartana Yoga (exchange yoga) is formed. This creates an extremely powerful connection between those two houses. The lords, though in different houses, function as if they are conjunct and mutually supporting each other. **BPHS 36.18** — The two most powerful Parivartana Yogas are: (1) Maha Parivartana — exchange between lords of Kendras and Trikonas creates powerful Raja Yoga effects, (2) Dainya Parivartana — exchange involving dusthana lords (6, 8, 12) weakens the natural quality of those houses involved. ## Amala Yoga **BPHS 36.19** — Amala Yoga is formed when the 10th house from Lagna or the Moon is occupied exclusively by natural benefics. The native acquires lasting fame, pure character, and prosperity throughout life. They are respected for ethical conduct and leave behind a positive legacy. ## Nipuna Yoga **BPHS 36.20** — When Mercury and Jupiter are conjunct in an auspicious house (1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th, 9th, 10th, 11th) or in mutual kendra relationship, Nipuna Yoga forms. The native exhibits exceptional intelligence, mastery in multiple fields, and a capacity for synthesis across disciplines. They become known as experts and advisors.

topic: Raja Yoga — Combinations for Authority and Power (part 1)
text: ## Foundation of Raja Yoga **BPHS 41.1** — Raja Yoga is formed when the lords of Kendras (1, 4, 7, 10) and Trikonas (1, 5, 9) are in mutual association by conjunction, aspect, or exchange. The native rises to positions of authority and power. The strongest Raja Yoga occurs when a single planet rules both a Kendra and a Trikona. **BPHS 41.2** — The Lagna (1st house) is simultaneously a Kendra and a Trikona — making the Lagna lord potentially the most powerful operator of Raja Yoga when it associates with other strong angular or trine lords. **BPHS 41.3** — Yogakaraka planets: For Taurus Lagna, Saturn rules the 9th (Capricorn) and 10th (Aquarius) — making Saturn the Yogakaraka (single-planet Raja Yoga). For Libra Lagna, Saturn rules the 4th (Capricorn) and 5th (Aquarius). For Cancer Lagna, Mars rules the 5th (Scorpio) and 10th (Capricorn) — making Mars the Yogakaraka. For Leo Lagna, Mars rules the 4th (Scorpio) and 9th (Aries). For Capricorn and Aquarius, Venus is Yogakaraka. **BPHS 41.4** — When the Yogakaraka planet is also well-placed (its own sign, exaltation, friend's sign) and in a Kendra or Trikona, the Raja Yoga becomes a Maha Raja Yoga — capable of elevating the native to the highest positions within their cultural and social context. ## Types of Raja Yoga **BPHS 41.5** — Kendra-Trikona Raja Yoga: When the 9th lord (most powerful trikona) aspects or conjoins the 10th lord (most powerful kendra), this creates the most potent classical Raja Yoga. The native rises to authority through dharmic (ethical) means and is respected for the combination of power and virtue. **BPHS 41.6** — Dhana Raja Yoga: When the 2nd lord, 11th lord, 5th lord, and 9th lord all associate with each other or with the Lagna lord, exceptional Dhana Raja Yoga forms. The native achieves both wealth and social prominence simultaneously. **BPHS 41.7** — Placement-based Raja Yoga: A Kendra lord placed in a Trikona, or a Trikona lord placed in a Kendra, qualifies as a Raja Yoga without requiring conjunction of their lords — placement alone in each other's domains creates the mutual reinforcement.

topic: Raja Yoga — Combinations for Authority and Power (part 2)
text: ## Specific Raja Yoga Examples from BPHS **BPHS 41.8** — For Aries Lagna: Jupiter (9th lord) with Mars (Lagna lord and 8th lord) creates Raja Yoga despite Mars's dusthana lordship; here Mars's identity as Lagna lord dominates. Venus (2nd and 7th lord) with Sun (5th lord) creates Dhana-Raja Yoga. **BPHS 41.9** — For Cancer Lagna: Mars (5th and 10th lord Yogakaraka) in any Kendra or Trikona naturally activates Raja Yoga conditions. Mars in Capricorn (exaltation) in the 7th house creates an exceptionally powerful expression — the native leads ambitious partnerships and enterprises. **BPHS 41.10** — For Scorpio Lagna: Jupiter (2nd and 5th lord) with Moon (9th lord) creates powerful Raja Yoga emphasizing fortune through knowledge and philosophy. The Jupiter-Moon combination for Scorpio ascendant is one of the most celebrated wealth-wisdom Raja Yogas. ## Timing of Raja Yoga Activation **BPHS 41.11** — Raja Yogas are activated primarily during the Mahadasha and Antardasha of the planets involved in creating the yoga. The yoga's effects are latent in the nativity but become manifest when the dasha period arrives. **BPHS 41.12** — The most powerful activation occurs when: (1) the Mahadasha planet is one of the Raja Yoga planets, (2) the Antardasha planet is another Raja Yoga planet, (3) transiting Jupiter or Saturn forms a supportive Gochara relationship with the natal Raja Yoga planets simultaneously. **BPHS 41.13** — Raja Yoga results also peak when the Progressed (secondary) Moon transits through the Raja Yoga planets' natal positions, or when the return of that planet to its natal sign (annual return) coincides with its Antardasha. ## Dhana Yoga (Wealth Combinations) **BPHS 41.14** — Dhana Yoga (wealth yoga) forms when the lords of the 2nd and 11th houses associate with the lords of the 5th, 9th, or ascendant by conjunction, aspect, or exchange. The 2nd house governs accumulated wealth; the 11th governs gains, income, and fulfillment of desires. **BPHS 41.15** — The combination of the 2nd, 5th, 9th, and 11th lords — all in mutual aspect or conjunction — is called Maha Dhana Yoga. The native accumulates extraordinary wealth through multiple streams, often from creative, educational, and

topic: Raja Yoga — Combinations for Authority and Power (part 3)
text: trading activities simultaneously. **BPHS 41.16** — A fully functioning Dhana Yoga activates most powerfully during the Mahadasha of the 2nd or 11th lord, especially when the 5th or 9th lord is the Antardasha planet. Financial windfall events, business breakthroughs, and inheritance often coincide with these dasha intersections.

## Overview of Vimshottari Dasha **BPHS 46.1** — The Vimshottari Dasha is a 120-year planetary period system based on the Moon's nakshatra at birth. The sequence: Ketu (7 years), Venus (20), Sun (6), Moon (10), Mars (7), Rahu (18), Jupiter (16), Saturn (19), Mercury (17). During each Mahadasha, the planet's house rulership, placement, dignity, and aspects determine life themes. **BPHS 46.2** — The nakshatra occupied by the Moon at birth determines which Mahadasha begins at birth and how much of that dasha's period remains. For example, if the Moon is in Ashwini (Ketu's nakshatra) at birth, the native begins in Ketu Mahadasha; the exact degree determines how much of the 7-year period remains. **BPHS 46.3** — The Antardasha (sub-period) lord modulates outcomes within each Mahadasha. If the Mahadasha and Antardasha lords are mutually friendly or rule benefic houses, the sub-period is favorable. If they are enemies or both rule dusthanas, challenges intensify during that Antardasha. ## Mahadasha Interpretations **BPHS 46.4 — Ketu Mahadasha (7 years)** — Ketu's period brings spiritual transformations, detachment, sudden changes, and past-life karma resolution. The native may face losses of material things but simultaneously gain deeper spiritual insight. Career may take unconventional directions. Health areas governed by Ketu (fevers, mysterious ailments, sudden events) become prominent. **BPHS 46.5 — Venus Mahadasha (20 years)** — The longest dasha, Venus brings pleasures, relationships, artistic pursuits, and material comforts. This period is generally favorable for marriage, business partnerships, financial accumulation through Venus-related fields (beauty, luxury, arts, entertainment). The dignity and house placement of natal Venus determines whether this largesse comes easily or with complications. **BPHS 46.6 — Sun Mahadasha (6 years)** — Sun's period activates themes of authority, self-expression, career recognition, and the father. The native often rises in status, takes leadership positions, or comes into conflict with authority figures depending on Sun's natal position. Government interactions become prominent. **BPHS 46.7 — Moon Mahadasha (10 years)** — Moon's period activates emotional needs, home and mother themes, public relations, and intuitive faculties. This is often a highly social period when the native comes to public attention. Domestically oriented and relationship-intensive; financial

results depend heavily on the Moon's natal placement and dignity. **BPHS 46.8 — Mars Mahadasha (7 years)** — Mars brings energy, courage, real estate, siblings, and technical pursuits to the foreground. This is often a highly productive if intense period — the native acts decisively and accomplishes objectives through determined effort. Conflicts with competitors are possible but usually resolved in the native's favor if Mars is well-placed. **BPHS 46.9 — Rahu Mahadasha (18 years)** — Rahu's long period creates sudden rises, unconventional opportunities, foreign connections, and obsessive focus on worldly achievement. The native may experience dramatic elevation in status followed by equally dramatic reversals if Rahu is afflicted. Foreign cultures, technology, and taboo subjects become fascinating. **BPHS 46.10 — Jupiter Mahadasha (16 years)** — Jupiter's period brings wisdom, dharmic elevation, spiritual teachers, beneficial long-distance travel, higher education, and good fortune through ethical means. The native often achieves recognition in advisory, educational, or philosophical roles. Marriage and children come easily during positive Jupiter dashas. **BPHS 46.11 — Saturn Mahadasha (19 years)** — Saturn's period requires patience, discipline, and structural work. This is rarely easy but almost always produces lasting achievements if the native persists. Career restructuring, health attention, and responsibility for others (parents, employees, the elderly) become central. The second half of Saturn dasha often brings harvest after the hard work of the first half. **BPHS 46.12 — Mercury Mahadasha (17 years)** — Mercury activates intellectual pursuits, communications, trade, siblings, short journeys, and writing. This period often sees the native flourish in fields requiring verbal, analytical, or mercantile skills. Education, business, and media involvement are prominent. ## Antardasha Timing **BPHS 46.13** — Within each Mahadasha, the sub-periods (Antardasha) of each planet occur in the Vimshottari sequence beginning with the Mahadasha lord itself. The duration of each Antardasha is proportional to the planet's Mahadasha length divided by 120, then multiplied by the Mahadasha duration. **BPHS 46.14** — The Mahadasha lord's own Antardasha (first sub-period) sets the tone for the entire Mahadasha. If this initial period is favorable, the rest of the Mahadasha tends to unfold positively. Challenges in the first Antardasha often

indicate a learning curve that resolves by the third or fourth sub-period. **BPHS 46.15** — Pratyantar Dasha (sub-sub-periods) provide the finest timing granularity for event prediction. Classical Jyotish focuses primarily on the interplay of three levels: Mahadasha, Antardasha, and Pratyantar dasha, cross-referenced with transits of slow-moving planets (Saturn, Jupiter, Rahu/Ketu). ## Transit Integration **BPHS 46.16** — Transit of the Mahadasha lord through its own sign, exaltation sign, or through the natal Lagna, Moon, or Mahadasha lord's natal position amplifies the dasha's effects significantly. Conversely, transit of the Mahadasha lord through dusthana positions from the Moon can bring challenges even in an otherwise good dasha. **BPHS 46.17** — Jupiter's transit through the 1st, 5th, 9th, 10th, and 11th houses from the natal Moon (Guru Transit) provides a strong positive modifier for the native's overall wellbeing and prosperity, regardless of which Mahadasha is running.

## Overview of Transit Interpretation **BPHS 65.1** — Transits (Gochara) are interpreted primarily from the natal Moon sign, as the Moon represents the mind, emotions, and daily experience. Transits from the Lagna are secondary but also valid, especially for physical and positional matters. The most important transiting planets are Jupiter (Brihaspati), Saturn (Shani), Rahu/Ketu (shadow planets), and the Sun. **BPHS 65.2** — The combination of Mahadasha/Antardasha and current transits together determine the actual timing and intensity of events. Even an excellent transit during a difficult dasha gives only partial relief; conversely, an excellent dasha during a challenging transit is moderated but still overall supportive. ## Saturn Transit — Sade Sati **BPHS 65.3** — When Saturn transits the 12th, 1st, and 2nd houses from the natal Moon (Sade Sati — seven and a half years total), the native experiences challenges related to health, career setbacks, emotional heaviness, and restructuring of life foundations. **BPHS 65.4** — The first phase of Sade Sati (Saturn in 12th from natal Moon) brings hidden expenses, isolation, sleep disturbances, foreign travel with mixed outcomes, and increasing awareness of what must be let go. Spiritual practice deepens during this phase. **BPHS 65.5** — The middle phase of Sade Sati (Saturn transiting directly over the natal Moon sign) is the most intense — bringing career crises, health problems, relationship difficulties, or family challenges. The mind feels burdened and clear decisions become harder. However, this is also the phase of deepest learning and transformation. **BPHS 65.6** — The final phase (Saturn in 2nd from natal Moon) affects finances, family relationships, and speech. The native may speak more bluntly, family relationships require attention, and financial restructuring is often necessary. However, this phase is also when the harvest of lessons from the entire Sade Sati begins to materialize. **BPHS 65.7** — For Capricorn and Aquarius Moon signs, Saturn is the lord of the Moon sign itself, so Sade Sati effects are significantly mitigated — the planet rules from its own domain and handles its transit with less friction. Similarly, those with strong Saturn in their natal chart (Capricorn, Aquarius, or Libra ascendant

with Saturn as Yogakaraka) handle Sade Sati more productively. **BPHS 65.8** — Remedies for Sade Sati include Saturn deity worship (Shani puja), lighting sesame oil lamps on Saturdays, charitable activities related to Saturn's significations (service to elderly, workers, outcasts), and wearing blue sapphire (Neelam) if astrologically confirmed by a qualified astrologer. ## Jupiter Transit — Guru Gochara **BPHS 65.9** — Jupiter transiting the 2nd, 5th, 7th, 9th, and 11th houses from the natal Moon gives favorable results — expansion of wealth, wisdom, relationships, fortune, and gains respectively. Jupiter transiting the 3rd, 6th, 8th, 10th, and 12th from Moon gives challenging results in those domains. **BPHS 65.10** — Jupiter's transit through a sign lasts approximately 12-13 months. It is most beneficial in direct motion and is weakened in retrograde or when combust by the Sun. The sign through which Jupiter transits determines which house it activates from different reference points. **BPHS 65.11** — Jupiter in the 11th from natal Moon (known as Labha Jupiter transit) is one of the best transit positions — it brings gains of all kinds, fulfillment of wishes, and a general sense of abundance and expansion. Major financial gains and positive career developments often coincide with this transit. **BPHS 65.12** — Jupiter's transit over the natal Lagna, natal Jupiter, or the 9th house (bhagya) from Lagna is exceptionally auspicious — often marking years of landmark achievement, recognition, marriage, or spiritual breakthrough. ## Rahu and Ketu Transits **BPHS 65.13** — Rahu and Ketu (lunar nodes) transit in retrograde motion, moving one sign approximately every 18 months. Rahu transiting the 1st, 3rd, 6th, 10th, and 11th houses from natal Moon generally gives material advancement through unconventional means. Ketu in the 3rd, 6th, 9th, and 12th can support spiritual progress. **BPHS 65.14** — Rahu's transit over the natal Moon creates intense mental agitation, obsessive thoughts, health challenges related to Rahu's significations (allergies, mysterious illnesses, anxiety), and sudden changes in circumstances. This is a period to avoid impulsive decisions. **BPHS 65.15** — The Rahu/Ketu axis transiting the 1st/7th or 4th/10th house axis from the Lagna often correlates with major relationship changes,

relocations, or career pivots. These transits activate the nodal axis in the chart, bringing fated encounters and karmic completions. ## Hora-Based Timing **BPHS 65.16** — For day-to-day timing precision, the hora system provides the finest granularity. Each hour of the day is ruled by a planet in the Chaldean order: Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Venus, Mercury, Moon. The first hora of sunrise belongs to the day-lord (Sunday → Sun, Monday → Moon, etc.). **BPHS 65.17** — For any lagna, the hora of the lagna lord, 5th lord, or 9th lord is considered auspicious for initiating important activities. The hora of the 6th, 8th, or 12th lord should be avoided for new ventures. The hora of the 10th lord is excellent for career matters. **BPHS 65.18** — Work begun during a beneficial hora and favorable choghadiya, when the transit lagna is also in a benefic house from the natal Lagna, has the best chance of successful initiation and completion. ## Choghadiya Muhurta **BPHS 65.19** — Choghadiya divides the day and night into 8 periods each (4 day choghadiyas + 4 night choghadiyas, each approximately 90 minutes for a 12-hour day/night cycle). The seven types: Amrit (Moon — excellent), Shubh (Jupiter — good), Labh (Mercury — gain), Char (Venus — moveable/average), Kaal (Saturn — inauspicious), Rog (Mars — diseased/avoid), Udveg (Sun — anxiety/avoid). **BPHS 65.20** — For important tasks, Amrit and Shubh choghadiyas are preferred. Labh is excellent for financial matters specifically. Char is neutral and acceptable for routine activities. Kaal, Rog, and Udveg should be avoided for initiating new work — though Rog is acceptable for medical treatments since Mars governs surgery and medicine.

topic: Pancha Mahapurusha Yogas — The Five Great Person Combinations (part 1)
text: ## Overview: Pancha Mahapurusha Yogas **BPHS 75.1** — The five Mahapurusha Yogas arise when Mars (Ruchaka), Mercury (Bhadra), Jupiter (Hamsa), Venus (Malavya), or Saturn (Shasha) occupies a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house) in its own sign or exaltation sign. Each yoga elevates the native to a distinguished position in their domain — military for Mars, commerce for Mercury, spirituality for Jupiter, arts for Venus, and discipline for Saturn. **BPHS 75.2** — The qualifier for all five Mahapurusha Yogas is identical: the planet must be (1) in a Kendra house from the Lagna, AND (2) in its own sign or exaltation sign. If either condition is absent, the yoga does not form. The strength of the Mahapurusha Yoga is further modified by the planet's freedom from combustion and Graha Yuddha (planetary war). **BPHS 75.3** — The results of each Mahapurusha Yoga peak during that planet's own Mahadasha period. However, even before the dasha activates, the yoga imbues the native's character with the planet's refined qualities from birth. ## Hamsa Mahapurusha Yoga (Jupiter) **BPHS 75.4** — When Jupiter occupies a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house) in its own sign (Sagittarius or Pisces) or exaltation sign (Cancer), Hamsa Yoga is formed. The native is righteous, learned, handsome in appearance, favored by rulers, and lives a long, prosperous life. **BPHS 75.5** — The Hamsa Yoga native is naturally inclined toward dharma, philosophy, and spiritual wisdom. They become teachers, scholars, counselors, and advisors. Their words carry authority and moral weight. People seek their guidance in times of crisis. **BPHS 75.6** — The physical characteristics attributed to Hamsa Yoga: a beautiful face like a lotus, a melodious voice like a swan (hamsa), a fair or golden-toned complexion, a well-proportioned body, and eyes that radiate calm wisdom. The native naturally attracts respect. **BPHS 75.7** — The strongest expression of Hamsa Yoga occurs when Jupiter is exalted in Cancer in the Kendra — especially in the Lagna (1st house) or 10th house. Jupiter exalted in the 1st house in Cancer gives the full splendor of the yoga — the native becomes a spiritual or

topic: Pancha Mahapurusha Yogas — The Five Great Person Combinations (part 2)
text: intellectual luminary of their generation. ## Malavya Mahapurusha Yoga (Venus) **BPHS 75.8** — When Venus occupies a Kendra in its own sign (Taurus or Libra) or exaltation sign (Pisces), Malavya Yoga is formed. The native possesses a strong physique, is wealthy, blessed with vehicles and comforts, learned, a connoisseur of arts, and enjoys a refined life. **BPHS 75.9** — The Malavya Yoga native is magnetic in personality, attracting devoted relationships and comfortable circumstances. They often achieve distinction in fields related to Venus — arts, beauty, entertainment, luxury goods, hospitality, fashion, and finance. **BPHS 75.10** — Physical characteristics of Malavya Yoga: a charming face, soft and melodious voice, well-shaped limbs, a liking for cleanliness and refined environments. The native instinctively creates aesthetic beauty wherever they go. ## Ruchaka Mahapurusha Yoga (Mars) **BPHS 75.11** — When Mars occupies a Kendra in its own sign (Aries or Scorpio) or exaltation sign (Capricorn), Ruchaka Yoga is formed. The native is brave, valorous, victorious over enemies, and will be a leader or commander. They possess a strong constitution, sharp features, and a commanding presence that naturally draws followers. **BPHS 75.12** — The Ruchaka Yoga native excels in fields requiring courage, quick action, and physical prowess — military, police, sports, surgery, engineering, and competitive entrepreneurship. They are decisive, direct, and unafraid of confronting adversaries. **BPHS 75.13** — The physical attributes of Ruchaka Yoga: athletic build, sharp features (especially nose and jawline), reddish or dark complexion, strong limbs, and an instinctive warrior stance. The native may have visible scars and occupies leadership positions naturally. ## Bhadra Mahapurusha Yoga (Mercury) **BPHS 75.14** — When Mercury occupies a Kendra in its own sign (Gemini or Virgo) or exaltation sign (Virgo), Bhadra Yoga is formed (exaltation and own sign coincide for Mercury in Virgo, making this a particularly powerful position). The native becomes eloquent, scholarly, business-minded, and achieves distinction through intellectual work. **BPHS 75.15** — The Bhadra Yoga native excels in communications, writing, commerce, analysis, teaching, and any field requiring quick wit and articulate expression. They synthesize information from multiple domains and communicate complex ideas clearly. Trading, consulting, and media

topic: Pancha Mahapurusha Yogas — The Five Great Person Combinations (part 3)
text: are natural domains. **BPHS 75.16** — Physical attributes: a well-proportioned nose, bright alert eyes, fair complexion, and quick graceful movements. The native speaks clearly and convincingly, with a natural gift for timing in conversation. ## Shasha Mahapurusha Yoga (Saturn) **BPHS 75.17** — When Saturn occupies a Kendra in its own sign (Capricorn or Aquarius) or exaltation sign (Libra), Shasha Yoga is formed. The native exhibits extraordinary discipline, endurance, organizational capability, and rises to positions of administrative power through patient persistent effort. **BPHS 75.18** — The Shasha Yoga native often rises to lead institutions, manage large organizations, govern territories, or build enduring systems through sheer persistence. They command through authority built over years, not charisma or luck. People trust them because they never abandon ship. **BPHS 75.19** — Physical attributes: dark or dusky complexion, strong bones and teeth, broad forehead, prominent facial structure, slow but deliberate gait. The native often appears older and more serious than their chronological age — this gives them early credibility and lasting authority. **BPHS 75.20** — The Shasha Yoga's results mature slowly — the first half of life builds the foundation; the remarkable achievements typically manifest after the age of 36 (considered Saturn's mature age). This yoga is powerful but requires patience for its full expression. ## Activation of Mahapurusha Yogas **BPHS 75.21** — All Mahapurusha Yoga results are activated most powerfully during the planet's own Mahadasha period. When the yoga-forming planet also happens to be a functional benefic (ruling a Kendra and/or Trikona) for the native's lagna, the results during its dasha period approach legendary levels of success. **BPHS 75.22** — Transits of the yoga-forming planet through its own exaltation or own sign during its Mahadasha create peak activation periods — sometimes lasting months at a time — where the native experiences optimal expression of the yoga's gifts. These transits should be timed carefully for important initiations, career moves, and major life decisions.

topic: Karaka System — Planetary Significators in Jaimini Jyotish (part 1)
text: ## Chara Karaka System **JAIMINI 1.1** — The seven Chara Karakas (variable significators) are assigned based on the longitude of each planet in the birth chart, from highest to lowest degree: Atmakaraka (AK), Amatyakaraka (AmK), Bhratrukaraka (BK), Matrukaraka (MK), Putrakaraka (PK), Gnatikaraka (GK), Darakaraka (DK). Rahu follows special rules and is typically excluded from the Chara Karaka scheme, or counted as the 8th karaka. **JAIMINI 1.2** — The Atmakaraka (AK) is the planet with the highest degree (ignoring sign) in the natal chart. It represents the soul's highest purpose — the primary karmic lesson the native must master in this lifetime. The Atmakaraka's natal placement, sign dignity, and dasha timing reveal the central arc of the life story. **JAIMINI 1.3** — The Darakaraka (DK) is the planet with the lowest degree in the natal chart. It governs the spouse and marriage partner. The sign containing the DK in D-9 (Navamsha), and the planets aspecting or joining the DK, describe the character and quality of the spouse. ## Jaimini Aspects **JAIMINI 1.4** — Jaimini aspects (Rashi Dristi) are sign-based, not degree-based. Fixed signs aspect all four cardinals. Cardinal signs aspect all four fixed. Dual signs aspect each other. This creates a different aspect grid from Parashari aspects, enabling unique timing calculations. **JAIMINI 1.5** — Fixed signs (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius) aspect all four cardinal signs (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn) but NOT each other or the dual signs. Cardinal signs (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn) aspect all four fixed signs but NOT each other or the dual signs. Dual signs (Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces) aspect each other only. ## Karakamsha Lagna **JAIMINI 1.6** — The Karakamsha Lagna is found by identifying the sign in D-9 (Navamsha) where the Atmakaraka falls. This becomes a special ascendant that reveals the soul's purpose, spiritual inclinations, and the field where the native will ultimately find fulfillment. Planets in Kendra from Karakamsha are particularly significant. **JAIMINI 1.7** — Jupiter in Kendra from Karakamsha gives the native a life oriented toward dharmic service, teaching, and wisdom. They become known as a wise advisor. Venus in Kendra from Karakamsha gives

topic: Karaka System — Planetary Significators in Jaimini Jyotish (part 2)
text: luxurious tastes, artistic refinement, and pleasurable marriage. Saturn in Kendra from Karakamsha makes the native a disciplined worker who rises through consistent effort. ## Padapancha (Pada Lagna) **JAIMINI 1.8** — Pada Lagna (Arudha Lagna, AL) is calculated as follows: count the number of signs the Lagna lord has moved from the Lagna, then count the same number of signs from the Lagna lord's position. The resulting sign is the Pada Lagna. This represents how the native is perceived by the world — their public image and reputation. **JAIMINI 1.9** — Benefics in the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th, 7th, 8th, 10th, 11th from the Arudha Lagna promote the public image and social status of the native. Malefics in these positions can damage reputation or create public adversaries, depending on which houses they rule. **JAIMINI 1.10** — The relationship between Lagna (soul) and Arudha Lagna (public image) reveals the alignment or disconnect between who the native truly is and how they are perceived. When both are in harmony (same sign, or in mutual Kendra/Trikona), the native's authentic self aligns with their public persona — creating trust and genuine influence.

topic: Yoga Combinations — Prosperity, Wisdom, and Fame (part 1)
text: ## Amala Yoga (Phaladeepika) **PHAL 6.1** — Amala Yoga (Pure Fame Yoga) arises when the 10th house from the Lagna or the Moon is exclusively occupied by natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, and a beneficially-associated Moon). This yoga generates fame that is both widespread and enduring — the native's reputation survives long after their physical life. **PHAL 6.2** — The specific quality of Amala Yoga is purity of means — the native achieves success without ethical compromise. Unlike some yogas that produce wealth through any means, Amala Yoga specifically indicates that the native's name is associated with noble deeds and righteous conduct. **PHAL 6.3** — Jupiter in the 10th from Lagna gives the highest expression of Amala Yoga — the native becomes a spiritual teacher, wise counselor, or philanthropist whose deeds inspire generations. Venus in the 10th gives artistic fame and renown in creative fields. Mercury in the 10th gives scholarly distinction and commercial success built on genuine expertise. ## Sunapha, Anapha, and Durudhara Yogas **PHAL 6.4** — Sunapha Yoga: When any planet other than the Sun occupies the 2nd house from the Moon (Moon's 2nd house), the native has self-earned wealth through personal capability and enterprise. The nature of the occupying planet determines the field of wealth. **PHAL 6.5** — Anapha Yoga: When any planet other than the Sun occupies the 12th house from the Moon, the native has natural charisma, a dignified bearing, and is admired by all. The native achieves renown for their character and disposition. **PHAL 6.6** — Durudhara Yoga: When planets occupy both the 2nd and 12th from the Moon (simultaneously creating both Sunapha and Anapha), Durudhara Yoga forms. The native enjoys the combined benefits — wealth and fame, enterprise and charisma, creating a well-rounded life of achievement. ## Shakata Yoga and Its Cancellation **PHAL 6.7** — Shakata Yoga forms when Jupiter is in the 6th, 8th, or 12th from the Moon (not in a Kendra from the Moon). This yoga can cause the native to face setbacks, reversals of fortune, and difficulty maintaining steady progress. Gains may come only to be followed by losses.

topic: Yoga Combinations — Prosperity, Wisdom, and Fame (part 2)
text: **PHAL 6.8** — Shakata Yoga cancellation (Shakata Bhanga): When Jupiter in a harmful position from the Moon is simultaneously in a Kendra or Trikona from the Lagna, the Shakata Yoga is cancelled. The native still experiences some fluctuation, but the overall life trajectory remains positive due to Jupiter's Lagna-relative beneficence. ## Chandra-Lagna Combinations **PHAL 6.9** — When the Moon and Lagna are the same sign (Moon in Lagna), the native has a strong, emotionally driven character — deeply identified with their emotional responses. The mind and body work in harmony, but the emotional patterns of early life are highly visible in the personality. **PHAL 6.10** — When the Moon is in a Kendra from the Lagna, the native possesses innate mental stability and the emotional capacity to handle public life effectively. This is considered highly auspicious for public roles, journalism, politics, and any occupation requiring public trust. **PHAL 6.11** — When the Moon and Lagna lord are in mutual Kendra or Trikona relationship, the native's emotions and identity work synergistically — creating a person whose inner life aligns with their outer expression. Such natives rarely experience the disconnect between public persona and private persona that plagues many.
